title: campanula sibirica subsp. divergentiformis
intro: Campanula sibirica subsp. divergentiformis, also known as Siberian bellflower, is a perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The plant's leaves and roots are rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds, which have been reported to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antioxidant activity. In herbal preparations, Campanula sibirica subsp. divergentiformis is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
